title: 5 Indian Air Force Pilots Are Dead. It Was a Training Flight.
script: 5 dead. A training flight? Yesterday morning at Jorhat Air Force Station in Assam, India, an AN-32 transport aircraft took off on a routine training sortie. Standard conditions. Ten a.m. departure. It never came back. The aircraft crashed at the station, and when rescuers reached the wreckage — visible as a plume of thick black smoke — 5 personnel were dead: Squadron Leader Prashant Singh, Flight Lieutenant Shubham Kumar, Sergeant Jitendra Sharma, Agniveervayu Khemaram Kumawat, and Agniveervayu Danish Alam. The co-pilot survived with injuries and is under treatment at an IAF facility. The Indian Air Force has flown AN-32s since 1984. A probe is underway. Could a 40-year-old aircraft on a routine morning flight be carrying this much risk?
